behaviors.description: A request whose signature does not match its body is rejected with 401 and not forwarded.
behaviors.justification: Pass 1 (INTENT TAG): module-handoff. Pass 2 (LABEL MAP): module-handoff->Integration. MOCK-MISS: failure is at boundary between HTTP raw-body extraction, signature verifier, and enqueue path; a pure function test can miss wiring bugs. REAL-INFRA: no real network/OS primitive required. LLM-DEP/MULTI-STEP: not applicable. REJECTION-GATE: rejected System because behavior text only requires reject/skip-forwarding, not real network or OS effects.
behaviors.test_description: Run handler with real middleware/module wiring (in-process HTTP test), send body+bad signature, assert 401 and assert queue write function/store was not invoked.

behaviors.description: An event is written to the persistent queue before HTTP 200 is returned.
behaviors.justification: Pass 1 (INTENT TAG): module-handoff. Pass 2 (LABEL MAP): module-handoff->Integration. MOCK-MISS: ordering bug appears in interaction between handler response path and queue persistence call. REAL-INFRA: can be caught with in-process wiring and local persistence adapter. LLM-DEP/MULTI-STEP: not applicable. REJECTION-GATE: rejected System because no real network/subprocess requirement is stated; requirement is ordering between local modules.
behaviors.test_description: Use integrated handler + queue adapter test double that records commit timestamp; issue request and assert queue write completes before response completion callback.

behaviors.description: Handler returns HTTP response without waiting for downstream event processing to finish.
behaviors.justification: Pass 1 (INTENT TAG): module-handoff. Pass 2 (LABEL MAP): module-handoff->Integration. MOCK-MISS: async decoupling failure appears at boundary between enqueue/ack path and worker processing trigger. REAL-INFRA: real OS/network not required to detect waiting vs non-waiting. LLM-DEP/MULTI-STEP: not applicable. REJECTION-GATE: rejected System because behavior does not require real external infrastructure, only async orchestration correctness.
behaviors.test_description: Wire handler with a downstream processor that blocks on a controllable promise; send request and assert response completes before unblocking processor.

behaviors.description: Response arrives within 3 seconds wall-clock even when earlier downstream processing is slow and concurrent.
behaviors.justification: Pass 1 (INTENT TAG): real-infra. Pass 2 (LABEL MAP): real-infra->System. MOCK-HIDE/REAL-INFRA: wall-clock latency under concurrency is sensitive to actual HTTP server, event loop scheduling, socket backlog, and runtime contention; in-process mocks can hide timeout/head-of-line failures. LLM-DEP/MULTI-STEP: not applicable. REJECTION-GATE: rejected Integration because behavior text explicitly requires wall-clock SLO under concurrent slow processing.
behaviors.test_description: Start service process with real HTTP listener and real worker concurrency settings; create slow in-flight workload, send new webhook, measure end-to-end response time, assert <3s.

behaviors.description: An event with an already-seen ID is not written to queue again.
behaviors.justification: Pass 1 (INTENT TAG): module-handoff. Pass 2 (LABEL MAP): module-handoff->Integration. MOCK-MISS: dedupe failures commonly appear in handler-to-persistent-store handoff (unique key/check-then-write race), not just local logic. REAL-INFRA: local persistent store wiring is sufficient; no external network/subprocess required. LLM-DEP/MULTI-STEP: not applicable. REJECTION-GATE: rejected System because requirement is duplicate prevention in queue write path, achievable with integrated local persistence.
behaviors.test_description: Use real queue persistence module (e.g., local DB/file-backed) with handler wiring; send same event ID twice, assert second request does not create second queued record.

behaviors.description: When queue is full, handler returns 503 and includes Retry-After header.
behaviors.justification: Pass 1 (INTENT TAG): module-handoff. Pass 2 (LABEL MAP): module-handoff->Integration. MOCK-MISS: failure boundary is translation of queue-capacity error into HTTP semantics. REAL-INFRA: can be validated with integrated handler and queue module/fake that emits full condition; no real network/subprocess required. LLM-DEP/MULTI-STEP: not applicable. REJECTION-GATE: rejected System because behavior text focuses on status/header mapping, not real OS/network characteristics.
behaviors.test_description: Wire handler to queue implementation configured at tiny capacity (or deterministic full signal), trigger full condition, assert HTTP 503 and `Retry-After` header presence/value.

behaviors.description: Persistent queue enforces configured capacity and rejects events beyond the limit.
behaviors.justification: Pass 1 (INTENT TAG): module-handoff. Pass 2 (LABEL MAP): module-handoff->Integration. MOCK-HIDE: mocked queue would always pass and hide real capacity enforcement defects in persistence layer. REAL-INFRA: requires real queue storage module behavior, but still local DB/filesystem class of infra, so Integration not System. LLM-DEP/MULTI-STEP: not applicable. REJECTION-GATE: rejected System because no real external API/network/subprocess is required by the behavior text; local persistence backend is enough.
behaviors.test_description: Instantiate actual persistent queue backend with capacity N, enqueue N events successfully, then enqueue N+1 and assert explicit rejection/error without increasing stored count.
